task_completion.description: Create a simulated physiological monitor providing SpO2 readings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an OpenICE device adapter window running a simulated physiological monitor or SpO2 device.

task_completion.description: Create a simulated controllable infusion pump.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an OpenICE device adapter window running a simulated controllable infusion pump.

task_completion.description: Launch the Infusion Safety clinical application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Infusion Safety' application open and visible within the OpenICE Supervisor interface.

task_completion.description: Configure the SpO2-based safety interlock threshold and ensure it is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Infusion Safety app interface for a specific SpO2 threshold value being set and an indicator showing the interlock/safety mechanism is active or enabled.

task_completion.description: Verify that both the SpO2 monitor and infusion pump are communicating with the safety application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for live data, connection statuses, or device identifiers for both the SpO2 device and the infusion pump displayed within the Infusion Safety application window.

task_completion.description: Write a technical configuration report documenting the setup and expected behavior.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor showing the file '/home/ga/Desktop/infusion_safety_config.txt'. The text must include the specific device types created, the SpO2 threshold value, and a description stating the pump will stop if SpO2 drops below the threshold.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the OpenICE GUI to create devices and launch the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenICE Supervisor and Device Adapter graphical windows being interacted with, rather than just terminal commands or a mock text file.

integrity.description: Verify the configuration report contains genuine details from the session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The device types and threshold value written in the text file should match the actual simulated devices and settings visible in the OpenICE GUI screenshots.

integrity.description: Verify the Infusion Safety application is genuinely running.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Infusion Safety app should appear as a functional component within the OpenICE Supervisor UI, displaying real-time UI elements, not a static image or generic text output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenICE is a real medical device interoperability platform. The task requires creating two simulated devices: an 'SpO2 monitoring device' and a 'controllable infusion pump'. The required application to launch is 'Infusion Safety', which implements a closed-loop safety system that stops medication infusion based on physiological status (e.g., when SpO2 drops below a configured threshold). The final output must be a text file at '/home/ga/Desktop/infusion_safety_config.txt' documenting the device types, SpO2 threshold, and expected system behavior (stopping the pump). Pass threshold is 60 points.
